---
title: db2pq
---

::: {.hero}
`db2pq` is a Python package for primarily built to support Parquet-centered workflows using data sourced from PostgreSQL data.
The original focus was on data from the PostgreSQL database offered by Wharton Research Data Services (WRDS).

`db2pq` is designed for a practical research workflow: move data out of WRDS or PostgreSQL, keep local Parquet copies current, and work with those files using tools like Polars, pandas, or R.

::: {.quick-links}
[Get Started](wrds-to-pq.qmd)
[Authentication](authentication.qmd)
[Use with Polars](using-parquet-with-polars.qmd)
[API Reference](reference/index.qmd)
:::
:::

## Installation

```bash
pip install --upgrade db2pq
```

Optional extras:

- `db2pq[sas]`: WRDS SAS-side metadata checks for refresh logic such as
  `wrds_update_pq(..., use_sas=True)`

```bash
pip install --upgrade "db2pq[sas]"
```

- `db2pq[pandas]`: pandas DataFrame outputs from metadata helpers such as
  `pq_last_modified(...)`

```bash
pip install --upgrade "db2pq[pandas]"
```

- `db2pq[ibis]`: export PostgreSQL-backed Ibis expressions with
  `ibis_to_pq(...)`

```bash
pip install --upgrade "db2pq[ibis]"
```

- `db2pq[adbc]`: use the Arrow-based PostgreSQL export path with
  `engine="adbc"`

```bash
pip install --upgrade "db2pq[adbc]"
```

## Quick example

```python
from db2pq import wrds_update_pq

wrds_update_pq("dsi", "crsp")
```

This example uses the standard update-aware WRDS-to-Parquet path.

## Main Paths

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### WRDS to Parquet

Export WRDS tables to Parquet files and update them only when the WRDS source
is newer.

[Open guide](wrds-to-pq.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### WRDS to PostgreSQL

Materialize WRDS tables into a local PostgreSQL database and manage indexes or
derived tables there.

[Open guide](wrds-to-pg.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### PostgreSQL to Parquet

Export local PostgreSQL tables or Ibis expressions into a Parquet repository.

[Open guide](pg-to-pq.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Parquet to PostgreSQL

Load Parquet repository tables back into PostgreSQL and refresh them when the
Parquet source is newer.

[Open guide](pq-to-pg.qmd)
:::
:::

## Learn More

- [Authentication](authentication.qmd): how `db2pq` finds WRDS credentials, uses
  `.env`, and works with `.pgpass`
- [Data management ideas](data-management.qmd): how to think about general-purpose
  versus project-level Parquet data
- [Using the WRDS web query](web-query.qmd): how to turn a WRDS web query into
  a cleaner SQL export workflow
- [Using the Parquet repository with Polars](using-parquet-with-polars.qmd):
  practical analysis patterns once the repository already exists
- [API Reference](reference/index.qmd): generated function reference from
  docstrings

## Docs Build

This site uses `quartodoc` for API pages and Quarto for rendering.
